title: Chapter 15 - Attributes
description: Questions about common HTML attributes and their purposes.

questions.prompt: What are id and class attributes used for?

options.label: Identifying elements for styling and scripting

explanations.overview: id and class are used to target elements from CSS and JavaScript.

byOptionId.identify: Correct — id and class let you select elements from CSS and JS.
byOptionId.content: Incorrect — attributes don't hold primary text content, elements do.
byOptionId.styleonly: Incorrect — attributes can be used by scripts and selectors, not just inline styles.
byOptionId.deprecated: Incorrect — id/class are standard attributes, not deprecated.

questions.prompt: Where do id and class attributes go?

options.label: Inside the closing tag of an HTML element
Between the opening and closing tags of an HTML element
Inside the opening tag of an HTML element
Before the opening tag of an HTML element

explanations.overview: id and class attributes go inside the opening tag of an HTML element.

byOptionId.start: Correct — id and class attributes go inside the opening tag.
byOptionId.end: Incorrect — id and class attributes do not go in the closing tag.
byOptionId.between: Incorrect — id and class attributes do not go between opening and closing tags.
byOptionId.before: Incorrect — id and class attributes do not go before the opening tag.
